description:
You are a compliance analyst who must create a short disk-usage audit trail for the security department.  
Using only standard GNU/Linux user-level tools, perform the following steps and save the combined output in a single plain-text log file.

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/audit_trails  
   if it does not already exist. (Use the default user permissions; no root privileges are required.)

2. Inside that directory create the file  
   /home/user/audit_trails/disk_usage_audit.log  

3. Write the exact text shown below so that the automated grader can match it precisely.  
   a. The first four logical lines (counting blank lines) **must** look exactly like this, including capitalisation and spacing:

      === DISK USAGE AUDIT TRAIL ===  
      Timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  
      
      1. Filesystem Summary (df -h)

      • “Timestamp:” must be followed by the current local date/time in the ISO-8601 **19-character** format “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS”.  
      • There must be a completely blank line after the timestamp line.  
      • The “1. Filesystem Summary …” line is the header that precedes the first command’s output.

4. Immediately after that header, append the complete, unmodified output of the command

      df -h

   (Do not filter or truncate; include the column headings and every subsequent line df prints.)

5. After the df output, add **one** blank line followed by the header

      2. Home Directory Size Breakdown (du -sh /home/user/*)

   Then append the full, unmodified output of

      du -sh /home/user/*

6. After the du output, add **one** blank line followed by the header  

      3. Inode Usage Summary (df -i)

   Then append the full, unmodified output of

      df -i

7. Do not add any extra text before, between or after the three numbered sections except for the single blank lines exactly as described.

8. When finished, you should be able to display the first 15 lines of the log with

      head -n 15 /home/user/audit_trails/disk_usage_audit.log

   and see the headers in the exact order shown in step 3.

This task is complete when:

• The file /home/user/audit_trails/disk_usage_audit.log exists.  
• The first four logical lines match the required template (header, timestamp line, blank line, section-1 header).  
• The three section headers appear in order, each preceded by exactly one blank line except the very first header.  
• Each section contains at least one data line produced by the corresponding command.  
• No additional headers, footers or commentary appear anywhere in the log.
truth:
Directory to be present before task: none (agent creates /home/user/audit_trails).

File that must exist after task:  
/home/user/audit_trails/disk_usage_audit.log

Mandatory fixed lines the grader will look for (1-based line numbers in the file):

Line 1: === DISK USAGE AUDIT TRAIL ===  
Line 2: Timestamp: <19-character-ISO-date-and-time> (regex `^Timestamp: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$`)  
Line 3: must be completely empty  
Line 4: 1. Filesystem Summary (df -h)  
After an arbitrary (≥1) number of lines containing df output, there must be exactly one empty line followed by:  
Header 2: 2. Home Directory Size Breakdown (du -sh /home/user/*)  
After an arbitrary (≥1) number of lines containing du output, there must be exactly one empty line followed by:  
Header 3: 3. Inode Usage Summary (df -i)

The grader will additionally check:

• The three headers appear in the correct order.  
• At least one non-blank, non-header line follows each header.  
• No extra headers beyond these three exist.  
• File permissions are regular user-writable (0600–0666).  
• Location is exactly /home/user/audit_trails/disk_usage_audit.log
